memoryArtifacts.headline: BALDWIN FAMILY HISTORY
memoryArtifacts.description:
This account by an unknown author was found among the documents owned by Irwin Rosa, 1925-2018


To extend a pedigree into the 16th Century is a challenge for most genealogists; particularly to carry …

Origin Displayed: Irish - Origins Available: English, Irish. Spelling variations of this family name include: Fitzgibbon, Fitzgibbons, MacGibbon, Gibbon, Gibbons, Gibben, Gibbens, Gibbin, Gibbins and many more. First found in county Mayo , where they had been granted land by Strongbow when he invaded Ireland in 1172. Some of the first settlers of this family name or some of its variants were: Daniel, George, John, Michael, James, Nicholas, and Patrick Fitzgibbon who all arrived in Philadelphia between 1840 and 1865; Ann Gibbon settled in Virginia in 1660. (From www.HouseOfNames.com Archives copyright  2000 - 2009). This interesting surname is of early medieval English origin, and is a patronymic form of Gibbon, which is a diminutive of Gibb, a pet form of the given name "Gilbert". Gilbert derives from "Gislebert", a Norman personal name composed of the Germanic elements "gisil", hostage, noble youth, and "berht", bright, famous. This given name enjoyed considerable popularity in England during the Middle Ages, partly as a result of the fame of St. Gilbert of Sempringham (1085 - 1189), the founder of the only native English monastic order. Richard Gibun is listed in the 1202 Pipe Rolls of Sussex. In some instances the surname may be patronymic from the Germanic personal name "Gebwine", from "geba", gift, and "wine", friend. Ralph Gibiun is noted in the 1176 Pipe Rolls of Oxfordshire. The full patronymic form of the name emerges in the 1379 Poll Tax Returns of Yorkshire, with the entry of one Roger Gibonson. On 30 April 1557, Richard, son of Hugh Gibbons, was christened at St. Michael Bassishaw, London. On 14 April 1635, James Gibbons, aged 21 yrs., embarked from London on the ship "Increase", bound for New England. A Coat of Arms granted to the family depicts a gold lion rampant, debruised by a silver bend charged with a torteau between two black crosses pattee fitchee on a red shield, the Crest being a red lion's gamb erased and erect charged with a bezant, holding a black cross pattee fitchee. The first recorded spelling of the family name is shown to be that of John Gibbons, jesuit, and doctor of philosophy at the German College, Rome, which was dated 1544, in the "Ecclesiastical Records of Rome", during the reign of King Henry 8th, known as "Bluff King Hal", 1509 - 1547. Surnames became necessary when governments introduced personal taxation. In England this was known as Poll Tax. Throughout the centuries, surnames in every country have continued to "develop" often leading to astonishing variants of the original spelling.  Copyright: Name Origin Research www.surnamedb.com 1980 – 2014.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Gibbons於1617年出生在英國瓦維其可爾分尼 康普頓時，她的父親Thomas Gibbons32歲，母親Elizabeth Pierpont30歲。她於1641年10月2日與Luke Hitchcock在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州哈特福德維瑟斯費爾德結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她於1636年移民到布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州哈特福德哈特福德。她去世於1696年4月25日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼哈普爾斯普林菲爾德享年79歲，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼哈普爾斯普林菲爾德斯普林菲爾德 其特利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Gibbons于1617年出生在英国瓦維其可爾分尼 康普頓时，她的父亲Thomas Gibbons32岁，母亲Elizabeth Pierpont30岁。她于1641年10月2日与Luke Hitchcock在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州哈特福德維瑟斯費爾德结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她于1636年移民到布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州哈特福德哈特福德。她去世于1696年4月25日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼哈普爾斯普林菲爾德享年79岁，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼哈普爾斯普林菲爾德斯普林菲爾德 其特利。

nameMeanings.name: Gibbons
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire): variant of Gibbon , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.
Irish (Mayo): in Ireland, the name is frequent and has been Gaelicized as Mac Giobúin. It is also found as a variant of Fitzgibbon .

variants.name: Gibbens
meanings: German: patronymic from a short form of an ancient Germanic personal name formed with geba ‘gift’.

variants.name: Gibbon
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Giboin (ancient Germanic Gebawin).
English: alternatively, a diminutive of the personal name   Gibb  .
Scottish and Irish: variant of   McGibbon  .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

historicEvents.title: Glorious Revolution
historicEvents.description: The Glorious Revolution brought the downfall of Catholic King James II and the reign of his Protestant daughter Mary and her Dutch husband, William III of Orange.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Gibbons was born in 1617, in Fenny Compton, Warwickshire, England, her father, Thomas Gibbons, was 32 and her mother, Elizabeth Pierpont, was 30. She married Luke Hitchcock on 2 October 1641, in Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. She immigrated to Hartford, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America in 1636. She died on 25 April 1696, in Springfield, Hampshire, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 79, and was buried in Springfield Cemetery, Springfield, Hampshire, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Gibbons est née en 1617, à Fenny Compton, Warwickshire, Angleterre, son père, Thomas Gibbons, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Pierpont, avait 30 ans. Elle a épousé Luke Hitchcock le 2 octobre 1641, à Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Elle a immigré au Hartford, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique en 1636. Elle est décédée le 25 avril 1696, à Springfield, Hampshire, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 79 ans, et a été enterrée à Springfield Cemetery, Springfield, Hampshire, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth Gibbons 1617 in Fenny Compton, Warwickshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Thomas Gibbons 32 und ihre Mutter Elizabeth Pierpont 30. Sie hat am 2. Oktober 1641 in Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America Luke Hitchcock geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie ist 1636 nach Hartford, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America eingewandert. Sie starb am 25. April 1696 im Alter von 79 Jahren in Springfield, Hampshire, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America und wurde in Springfield Cemetery, Springfield, Hampshire, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Gibbons è nata il nel 1617 nel Fenny Compton, Warwickshire, England, sua padre, Thomas Gibbons, aveva 32 anni e sua madre, Elizabeth Pierpont, aveva 30 anni. Lei sposato Luke Hitchcock il 2 ottobre 1641, nel Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. Immigrata nel Hartford, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America nel 1636. Morì il 25 aprile 1696, nel Springfield, Hampshire, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, all'età di 79 anni, e fu sepolta a Springfield Cemetery, Springfield, Hampshire, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Gibbonsが1617年にイギリスワーウィクシュアイアフエニー コンプトンで生まれた時，父Thomas Gibbonsは32歳，母Elizabeth Pierpontは30歳であった。1641年10月2日にイギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ハートフォードウエスエルスフィールドでLuke Hitchcockと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1636年にイギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ハートフォードハートフォードに移住した。彼女は1696年4月25日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーハンプシュアイアスプリングフィールドで79歳で死去し，イギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーハンプシュアイアスプリングフィールドスプリングフィールド セメテリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Gibbons(이)가 1617년 영국 워윅실르 펜이 콤프턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Gibbons(은)는 32세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Pierpont(은)는 30세였다. 그녀는 1641년 10월 2일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 하트퍼드 w더스필드에서 Luke Hitchcock(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1636년에 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 하트퍼드 하트퍼드로 이민을 떠났다. 그녀는 1696년 4월 25일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 함프실르 스프링필드에서 79세의 나이로 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 함프실르 스프링필드 스프링필드 세멧리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Gibbons nasceu em 1617, em Fenny Compton, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas Gibbons, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Pierpont, tinha 30 anos. Ela casou-se com Luke Hitchcock em 2 de outubro de 1641, em Wethersfield, Hartford, Conecticute, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ela imigrou para o Hartford, Hartford, Conecticute, British Colonial America em 1636. Ela faleceu em 25 de abril de 1696, em Springfield, Hampshire, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, com 79 anos, e foi sepultada em Springfield Cemetery, Springfield, Hampshire, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Gibbons родилась в 1617 г. в местности фенни комптон, варвиккшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Thomas Gibbons было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Pierpont, 30. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Luke Hitchcock 2 октября 1641 г. в местности ветирсфиелд, хартфорд, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Она иммигрировала в хартфорд, хартфорд, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика в 1636 году. Она умерла 25 апреля 1696 г. в местности спрингфиелд, хампшир, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 79 лет и была похоронена в местности спрингфиелд кеметири, спрингфиелд, хампшир, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth Gibbons nació en 1617, en Fenny Compton, Warwickshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Thomas Gibbons, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Pierpont, tenía 30 años. Se casó con Luke Hitchcock el 2 de octubre de 1641, en Wethersfield, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. Inmigró a Hartford, Hartford, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America en 1636. Murió el 25 de abril de 1696, en Springfield, Hampshire, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 79 años, y fue sepultada en Springfield Cemetery, Springfield, Hampshire, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.
